title: doctrine sync: orchestrator-claims-before-spawn in skill text + final render/manifest (D1+D3 close)
action: Per D1/D3: update worker + orchestrator skill text — orchestrator claims the cell (claim-next or cells claim --id, session id self-derived or explicit) BEFORE spawning; worker startup VALIDATES ownership via cells show and never runs cells claim; reservation instructions drop handed-down session ids (auto-derive; a worker may read its own CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID). Keep AO14 single-execution-worker contract wording intact. Introduce NONE of the stale tokens census_stale_spawn_syntax pins (spawn_agent(agent_type / fork_context / re-spawn continuation). PLUS (plan-check C6): append the four new suites — test_store_lock, test_claim_race, test_reservation_race, test_heartbeat_touch — into .bee/config.json commands.verify so they survive as regression, not just cell caps. Final release_manifest --write + --check. Re-render skill projections only if test_skill_render demands. This cell is docs/skill-text/config only — no lib edits.

truths: no skill text instructs a worker to run cells claim
no literal handed-down session id remains in worker templates

verify: node scripts/test_skill_render.mjs && node scripts/test_conformance.mjs && node scripts/census_stale_spawn_syntax.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Skill text (bee-executing SKILL.md + worker-details.md, bee-swarming SKILL.md + swarming-reference.md) now describes D1/D3 reality: orchestrator claims (claim-next or cells claim --id, session self-derived) BEFORE spawning; workers validate ownership via cells show and never run cells claim; no literal session id is handed down in worker templates. Appended test_store_lock/test_claim_race/test_reservation_race/test_heartbeat_touch to .bee/config.json commands.verify per plan-check C6. release_manifest --write + --check regenerated clean (364 files).
deviations: Out-of-scope discovery, not fixed here: scripts/test_claim_race.mjs scenario (c) (same-session round-trip claim->block->reopen->claim) throws `blockCell: cell "race-c" is claimed by session "sess-roundtrip" ... another session owns it` because msh-4's D4 claim-ownership guard now checks blockCell/reopenCell callers, but the test (written by msh-2, before D4 existed) calls both without a matching sessionId. msh-4's own verify chain (test_lib.mjs + test_bee_cli.mjs + test_lib_mirror.mjs + release_manifest --check) never re-ran this msh-2 suite, so the regression was uncaught until this cell wired test_claim_race.mjs into .bee/config.json commands.verify per its assigned action. test_store_lock.mjs, test_reservation_race.mjs, and test_heartbeat_touch.mjs all run fresh-green. Not fixed in this cell: scripts/test_claim_race.mjs is outside the cell's files scope (docs/skill-text/config only, no lib/test edits) — needs its own fix-first tiny cell (a one-line same-session sessionId fix in the test) before the next full commands.verify run reports green.
trace.friction: out-of-scope but important: wiring test_claim_race.mjs into commands.verify (per assigned action) surfaced a pre-existing D1-vs-D4 regression in that test (see deviations) — needs a fix-first tiny cell; not fixed here, outside this cell's file scope.
